scope.target: v0.6.0 product, distribution, customization, software-delivery, and naming decisions
constraints: Do not allocate proposed backlog IDs before approval
Do not treat planning persistence as implementation authorization
Keep backlog optional and software-delivery workflows core
Keep target-facing governance, auditor, and upgrader customization safety core
non_goals: Change package bytes during the planning checkpoint
Rename a skill before compatibility and evaluation decisions
Move AI-CONTEXT-SOURCE.yaml without an approved migration
Implement CI, CFG, taxonomy, evaluation, or simplification work

steps: Deliberate DIST-001 product and package composition
Deliberate semantic customization records and provenance placement
Normalize only approved decisions into backlog and roadmap
execution.last_completed_step: Allocated CUST-001 and DEVWF-002 from the approved semantic customization, historical dev-workflow, test execution, selectable spec compliance, and commit-density decisions
execution.next_action: Design the exact DIST-001, CUST-001, and DEVWF-002 implementation workflows and downstream fixtures before changing package bytes or active skill contracts

results.summary: The v0.6.0 product plan now has explicit release blockers for one target-owned semantic customization ledger and software-development end-to-end acceptance. DEVWF-002 records v0.0.2/v0.0.3 history, intent-based activation, approval pauses, stage-batched commits, pluggable unit/integration test execution without a new skill, conditional specialized tests, and selectable spec compliance.
results.residual_risk: Owner decisions and detailed backlog contracts are preserved, but schemas, active-context normalization, package projection, migration, and downstream fixtures remain unimplemented. The workplace-specific dev-workflow lineage and any external test skill require later evidence.
